name: Paul Gonsalves
abstract: Paul Gonsalves ((1920-07-12)July 12, 1920 – (1974-05-15)May 15, 1974) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist best known for his association with Duke Ellington. At the 1956 Newport Jazz Festival, Gonsalves played a 27-chorus solo in the middle of Ellington's "Diminuendo and Crescendo in Blue", a performance credited with revitalizing Ell…

works.t: Things Ain't What They Used To Be
Second Sacred Concert: The Shepherd (Who Watches Over the Night Flock)

concerts.label: Duke Ellington in His Concert of Sacred Music

about_quotes.speaker: Norris Turney
about_quotes.evidence: Harry Carney, Russell Procope, Harold Ashby, Paul Gonsalves and me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: He was using, as his personnel for this record, the Ellington band, everybody except Duke of course... So we had all of us – [Johnny] Hodges, Paul Gonsalves, Jimmy Woode, Sam Woodyard, Jimmy Hamilton. We were all on his date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: That was the night that we did "Diminuendo and Crescendo in Blue and Paul Gonsalves went out there and played 27 choruses.

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: And myself, that was the trumpet section and Dizzy of course playing everything... Paul Gonsalves is playing tenor.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: After that, I roomed with Tizol a couple of times, and Paul Gonsalves.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I found after working with the band I discovered in a very short time that you could have Ellington’s band play an arrangement... Carney, Willie Smith, Johnny Hodges, Paul Gonsalves, Jimmy Hamilton, Clark Terry, Juan Tizol, Lawrence Brown, Tricky Sam.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
